campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to hear a financial report from the Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority covering March 2026 investments, approve a new policy restricting use of city property for federal civil enforcement activities, and discuss efforts to fill open city job positions and improve employee retention.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority monthly investment report for March 2026
New city policy prohibiting federal civil enforcement use of city-owned property
Campbell city job vacancies and strategies to recruit and retain employees

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing about cleaning up hazardous conditions on certain properties that the County identified. They also adopted new policies on technology disruption and set up a process for residents to request accommodations if they need special help from the city.
keyTopics: Hazardous property cleanup and removal orders
New city technology policy to replace old rules
Process for residents to request reasonable accommodations

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — May 5, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met to approve previous meeting minutes and address vegetation management. The council declared overgrown brush and hazardous vegetation a public nuisance problem, approved an engineer's report for landscape and lighting improvements, and authorized the Town Manager to sign multi-year service agreements with contractors.
keyTopics: Hazardous brush and overgrown vegetation declared a public nuisance
Landscape and lighting improvements approved by engineers
Five-year service contracts authorized with vendors
April 21 meeting minutes approved

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 13, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize upcoming council business, including finalizing the agenda for May 19 and setting the meeting schedule for fiscal year 2026-2027. The council also made appointments to various city committees, boards, and commissions, and approved a travel request.
keyTopics: Finalized May 19 council agenda with scheduled items and timing
Approved fiscal year 2026-2027 city council meeting schedule
Made council appointments to committees, boards, and commissions
Approved travel request from Councilmember Foley

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council is inviting residents to submit questions before their May 14, 2026 meeting. The council is holding meetings for various boards, commissions, and committees, with specific locations posted at least 24 hours in advance.
keyTopics: Submit public questions online before the meeting
Board, commission, and committee meeting locations posted in advance
Contact City Manager's Office at 650-903-6301 or CNC@MountainView.gov with agenda questions

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 5, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-session meeting on May 5, 2026, followed by a regular council meeting. The specific matters discussed in the closed session were not publicly detailed, as these sessions are held privately.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting held (details not disclosed to public)
Regular council meeting convened
Small business matters on agenda

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 11, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council held an initial planning session to review Cupertino's proposed budget for fiscal year 2026-27. The meeting also covered requirements for lobbyists to register and report their activities when they try to influence city decisions.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget proposal review and discussion
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under city law
Public participation in legislative and administrative processes

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 14, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on May 14, 2026 included a disclosure about legal deadlines for residents who want to challenge city decisions. If the city makes a quasi-adjudicative decision (like approvals or denials on permits or land-use matters), residents have 90 days from the final decision to file a lawsuit, unless state law provides a shorter timeframe.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue the city over quasi-adjudicative decisions
Legal challenge procedures governed by California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: Milpitas City Council met to approve the city's five-year capital improvement plan and discuss the next year's operating budget. The council also adopted a new parks and trails master plan for the Innovation District and updated internal meeting procedures.
keyTopics: 2027-2031 Capital Improvement Plan approved
FY 2026-27 Operating Budget presentation and direction
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan adopted
City Council procedures and protocols updated

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a hearing on a proposal to build a 6-story apartment building with 72 units on the city-owned parking lot at 450 Lytton Avenue. All units would be permanently affordable housing, and the developer is requesting special approvals under state law to allow the denser construction on this small parcel.
keyTopics: 72 affordable housing units proposed at 450 Lytton Avenue
6-story building on 0.43-acre city parking lot (replacing 52 parking spaces)
Request for density bonus concessions and waivers under state housing law
Environmental review process underway
